stem: For the linear Diophantine equation \( [[A]]x + [[B]]y = c \), what is the smallest positive integer value of \( c \) for which the equation has a solution in integers \( x \) and \( y \)?
solution: The equation \( [[A]]x + [[B]]y = c \) has an integer solution iff \( \gcd([[A]], [[B]]) \) divides \( c \). The smallest positive multiple of the gcd is the gcd itself, so \( c = \gcd([[A]], [[B]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( [[A]] + [[B]] \); it is representable but is not the smallest positive value.
This is a common multiple of the coefficients, not the smallest representable value.
This is one more than the gcd.
This is one less than the gcd.
This is two more than the gcd.
This is two less than the gcd.
This is three more than the gcd.

stem: Suppose \( [[A]] \) and \( [[B]] \) are coprime positive integers. What is the largest positive integer \( c \) for which \( [[A]]x + [[B]]y = c \) has NO solution in non-negative integers \( x, y \)?
solution: For coprime \( A,B \), the Chicken McNugget (Frobenius) theorem gives the largest non-representable positive integer (using non-negative \( x,y \)) as \( AB - A - B = [[A]]\cdot[[B]] - [[A]] - [[B]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the product \( AB \) itself, which IS representable (\( x=B, y=0\) or similar).
This is just the sum of the two coefficients, not the Frobenius number.
This subtracts only one of the two coefficients from the product.
This subtracts only the other coefficient from the product.
This is one more than the correct Frobenius number.
This is one less than the correct Frobenius number.
This is two more than the correct Frobenius number.

stem: For the equation \( [[A]]x + [[B]]y = c \) with integers \( x,y \) (not necessarily non-negative), how many positive integers \( c \) with \( 1 \le c \le [[N]] \) admit a solution?
solution: A value \( c \) is representable exactly when \( \gcd([[A]], [[B]]) \) divides \( c \); the number of multiples of \( \gcd([[A]],[[B]]) \) in \( [1,[[N]]] \) is \( \lfloor [[N]]/\gcd([[A]],[[B]]) \rfloor = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes every value up to \( N \) is representable, ignoring the gcd condition.
This is the gcd itself, not the count of multiples of it up to \( N \).
This counts multiples of \( [[A]] \) instead of the (smaller) gcd.
This counts multiples of \( [[B]] \) instead of the (smaller) gcd.
This is one more than the correct count.
This is one less than the correct count.
This is two more than the correct count.

stem: The equation \( [[A]]x + [[B]]y = [[c]] \) has an integer solution if and only if \( \gcd([[A]],[[B]]) \) divides \( [[c]] \). What is the remainder when \( [[c]] \) is divided by \( \gcd([[A]],[[B]]) \)? (A remainder of 0 means the equation IS solvable.)
solution: Compute \( g = \gcd([[A]],[[B]]) \), then reduce \( [[c]] \) modulo \( g \): the remainder is \( [[answer]] \); a remainder of 0 would mean \( g \mid [[c]] \) and the equation is solvable in integers.
distractor_rationales: This reduces \( c \) modulo \( [[A]] \) instead of modulo the gcd.
This reduces \( c \) modulo \( [[B]] \) instead of modulo the gcd.
This is the gcd itself, not the remainder of \( c \) upon division by it.
This reduces \( c \) modulo the shared factor \( [[d]] \) alone rather than the full gcd \( [[A]],[[B]] \) share.
This is one more than the correct remainder.
This is one less than the correct remainder.
This is two more than the correct remainder.

stem: Suppose \( [[A]] \) and \( [[B]] \) are coprime positive integers. Using ONLY non-negative integers \( x,y \), how many positive integers \( c \) can NEVER be written as \( [[A]]x + [[B]]y \)?
solution: By Sylvester's theorem, for coprime \( A,B \) the TOTAL number of positive integers not representable as a non-negative combination \( Ax+By \) is exactly \( \dfrac{(A-1)(B-1)}{2} = \dfrac{([[A]]-1)([[B]]-1)}{2} = [[answer]] \) (half of all the "gaps", the other half being representable, up to and beyond the Frobenius number).
distractor_rationales: This is the Frobenius number (the LARGEST non-representable value), not the total COUNT of non-representable values.
This is the product \( AB \), an unrelated quantity.
This forgets to halve the product of the shifted factors.
This is just the sum of the two coefficients, not the gap count.
This is one more than the correct count.
This is one less than the correct count.
This is two more than the correct count.
